hooks.text: A two percent gap on one supplier wire feels too small to chase. It's also the reason a business owner can lose twelve thousand dollars in a year without one fee on a single receipt.
Two percent on one wire to your supplier looks like nothing. Run the same wire twelve times a year and something happens that no business owner sees coming.
Spend five hundred thousand dollars sourcing this year at a two percent rate gap, and ten thousand dollars quietly stays with your bank. No fee line, no notification.
A business owner shrugged off a two percent gap on every supplier wire for a year. Same supplier, same orders, twelve thousand dollars he never accounted for, gone in the rate.
Every wire to your Chinese supplier carries a two percent rate gap your bank keeps. On fifty thousand dollars a wire, twelve wires a year, that's twelve thousand dollars lost.
